---
name: Methodology discussion
about: Propose a change to the rubric, weights, lens definitions, or calibration anchors
title: "[methodology] "
labels: methodology
assignees: ''
---

Methodology changes are NOT made by patch-level edit. They go through a structured revision process. This template starts that conversation.

## What part of the methodology

- [ ] Category structure (the 13 top-level categories)
- [ ] Sub-criterion definitions
- [ ] Default weights (`docs/methodology/spec-v1.2-redlined.md` §6)
- [ ] Lens definitions or lens weight vectors
- [ ] Era benchmarks (`docs/methodology/era-benchmarks-v1.md`)
- [ ] Calibration anchors (FDR, Truman, Eisenhower, Nixon, Reagan)
- [ ] Other:

## What you propose

<!-- Specific change. What is, what should be, why. -->

## Evidence or precedent

<!-- Published presidential-rankings methodologies (C-SPAN, APSA, Siena, Brookings/Miller Center) that support the change, academic literature, or structural arguments. -->

## Expected effect on existing scores

<!-- If the change re-ranks presidents materially, name which ones and how. -->

## Anything else

---

Methodology proposals are evaluated against the published-methodology defense in `docs/methodology/weight-validation-v1.md` and the era-anchor logic in `docs/methodology/spec-v1.2-redlined.md` §4.5. Acceptance is editorial, not democratic.
